title: feuilleea cochleata
intro: Feuilleea cochleata, also known as Snail shell vine, is a perennial climber native to Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Feuilleea cochleata include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Feuilleea cochleata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used topically as a poultice or ointment.
